ddls:
CREATE TABLE "collections" (
  "collection_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each collection */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the collection */,
  "poster_path" TEXT /* Path to the poster image of the collection */,
  "backdrop_path" TEXT /* Path to the backdrop image of the collection */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the collection */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the collection */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the collection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("collection_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_genres" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* ID of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "keywords" (
  "keyword_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each keyword */,
  "keyword" TEXT /* Keyword associated with the movie */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("keyword_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_keywords" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "keyword_id" INTEGER /* ID of the keyword */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "keyword_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movies_collections" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "collection_id" INTEGER /* ID of the collection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "collection_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "casts" (
  "cast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cast entry */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "actor_name" TEXT /* Name of the actor */,
  "character_name" TEXT /* Name of the character played by the actor */,
  "character_gender" TEXT /* Gender of the character */,
  "character_age" INTEGER /* Age of the character */,
  "role_type" TEXT /* Type of role (e.g., Lead, Supporting) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cast_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "directors" (
  "director_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each director entry */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "director_name" TEXT /* Name of the director */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the director (e.g., director, co-director) */,
  "director_gender" TEXT /* Gender of the director */,
  "director_age" INTEGER /* Age of the director */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("director_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, user, guest) */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* Date when the user joined the platform */,
  "last_active" TEXT /* Date of the user's last activity */,
  "profile_picture" TEXT /* Path to the user's profile picture */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie being rated */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who rated the movie */,
  "rating" REAL /* Rating given by the user */,
  "rating_date" TEXT /* Date when the rating was given */,
  "reviewed" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the user has also written a review for the movie */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each review */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie being reviewed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who wrote the review */,
  "review_text" TEXT /* Text of the review */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date when the review was written */,
  "rating" REAL /* Rating given by the user in the review (if any) */,
  "spoiler_flag" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the review contains spoilers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_preferences" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "preferred_genres" TEXT /* Preferred genres of the user */,
  "preferred_actors" TEXT /* Preferred actors of the user */,
  "preferred_directors" TEXT /* Preferred directors of the user */,
  "preferred_languages" TEXT /* Preferred languages of the user */,
  "preferred_countries" TEXT /* Preferred countries of the user */,
  "preferred_certifications" TEXT /* Preferred certifications of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
